# voxctrl — Intelligent Audio Interface for Linux
# llms.txt — structured index for AI agent discovery
# https://llmstxt.org/

> voxctrl is a programmable voice input broker for Linux. It transcribes speech locally using
> faster-whisper (NVIDIA CUDA), whisper.cpp (AMD/Intel Vulkan), or Moonshine (streaming ONNX, CPU/edge),
> routes the transcript to
> named output targets (inject, exec, pipe, socket, file, dbus), applies per-target AI post-processing
> pipelines via local Ollama models, and exposes a built-in MCP server so any MCP-capable AI agent
> (Claude, Cursor, Zed) can use the microphone and TTS as first-class tools.

## Quick Facts

- License: MIT
- Platform: Linux (Wayland-native; X11 supported)
- Language: Python, PyQt6
- Repository: https://github.com/JRufer/voxctrl
- MCP Server: Unix domain socket at /tmp/voxctrl-mcp.sock (JSON-RPC 2.0)
- Config: ~/.config/voxctrl/config.json, targets.toml, bindings.toml

## Key Capabilities

1. **Local transcription** — faster-whisper, whisper.cpp, or Moonshine; auto-selects backend from GPU/hardware
2. **Programmable routing** — 7 output target types (inject/clipboard/exec/pipe/socket/file/dbus)
3. **Per-target AI pipelines** — strip_fillers, ollama_prompt, code_mode, snippets, spoken_punct
4. **MCP server** — transcribe_voice, speak_text, get_status tools over Unix socket
5. **Code Mode** — IDE-aware voice dictation; camelCase/snake_case spoken identifier formatting
6. **AT-SPI2 injection** — direct text insertion into focused widgets; reads context before cursor
7. **Neural TTS** — Piper on-device speech synthesis; 8 curated English voice models
8. **Visual overlays** — Voice Card (default), Waveform, Pulse Circle; custom Python plugins

## MCP Integration

Server name: voxctrl
Socket: /tmp/voxctrl-mcp.sock
Protocol: JSON-RPC 2.0, newline-delimited

### Tools

| Tool               | Signature                           | Description                                                  |
|--------------------|-------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| transcribe_voice   | timeout_seconds?: number → string   | Opens mic, returns transcript after VAD silence or timeout   |
| speak_text         | text: string → "spoken"             | Queues text for Piper TTS; non-blocking                      |
| get_status         | → {recording: bool, speaking: bool} | Returns current audio I/O state                              |

### Claude Desktop config

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "voxctrl": {
      "command": "socat",
      "args": ["STDIO", "UNIX-CONNECT:/tmp/voxctrl-mcp.sock"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Configuration Files

### config.json (global settings)

Key options: whisper_model, tts_enabled, tts_voice, mcp_server_enabled, atspi_injection,
atspi_auto_code_mode, show_overlay, overlay_style, vad_threshold.

### targets.toml (output destinations)

Each [[target]] entry defines: id, label, delivery, post_processing, and optional [target.ollama].
Delivery types: inject | clipboard | exec | pipe | socket | file | dbus

Example:
```toml
[[target]]
id              = "hermes"
label           = "Hermes Agent"
delivery        = "pipe"
pipe_path       = "/tmp/hermes.in"
post_processing = "strip_fillers"
response_pipe   = "/tmp/hermes.out"
append_newline  = true
```

### bindings.toml (hotkey gestures)

Each [[binding]] maps keys + gesture to a target ID.
Gesture types: hold | toggle | double_tap

Example:
```toml
[[binding]]
keys    = ["KEY_LEFTMETA", "KEY_SPACE"]
gesture = "hold"
target  = "focused_window"
```

- Tutorials: https://voxctrl.dev/docs/tutorials
- Architecture: https://voxctrl.dev/docs/architecture

## Hardware Backends

| Hardware              | Backend           | Acceleration          |
|-----------------------|-------------------|-----------------------|
| NVIDIA GPU (CUDA11+)  | faster-whisper    | CUDA                  |
| AMD GPU (RDNA/GCN)    | whisper.cpp       | Vulkan                |
| Intel Arc / Iris Xe   | whisper.cpp       | Vulkan                |
| CPU only              | faster-whisper    | int8 fallback         |
| Raspberry Pi / Edge   | moonshine         | ONNX int8 (CPU-first) |

## AI Pipeline Stages

strip_fillers | spoken_punct | snippets | ollama_prompt | prepend | append_newline | code_mode

## Security Model

- Unix socket only by default (no network exposure)
- Zero outbound traffic (all processing is local)
- Optional microphone confirmation (require_confirmation: true)
- Tool allowlist: disable individual MCP tools via config
- Target allowlist: restrict which targets MCP clients can route to
